questions.q: In Hindu tradition, the serpent Vasuki is honoured as the king of which class of beings?
questions.explanation: Vasuki is revered as the king of the Nagas, the divine serpent race of Hindu lore.

questions.q: According to the Puranas, around whose neck does the serpent Vasuki coil as an ornament?
questions.explanation: Vasuki is famously depicted coiled around the neck of Shiva as a living ornament.

questions.q: In the Samudra Manthana, what role did the serpent Vasuki serve for the devas and asuras?
questions.explanation: Vasuki was used as the great rope wound around Mount Mandara to churn the ocean of milk.

questions.q: Which mountain did the gods and demons wind the serpent Vasuki around during the churning of the ocean of milk?
questions.explanation: In the Samudra Manthana, Vasuki was coiled about Mount Mandara, which served as the churning rod.

questions.q: What kind of creature is Vasuki in Hindu mythology?
questions.explanation: Vasuki is a Naga, a divine serpent, and is counted as a king among the serpent race.

questions.q: During the churning of the ocean, the serpent Vasuki disgorged a deadly substance. What was it?
questions.explanation: Strained in the churning, Vasuki disgorged the terrible halahala poison that threatened all creation.

questions.q: According to the Puranas, which deity drank the halahala poison released by the serpent Vasuki to save creation?
questions.explanation: Shiva drank the halahala poison, holding it in his throat, which is why he is called Nilakantha, the blue-throated one.

questions.q: The serpent Vasuki appears prominently in which famous episode of Hindu mythology?
options: The churning of the ocean of milk
questions.explanation: Vasuki is central to the Samudra Manthana, the churning of the ocean of milk, where he served as the churning rope.

questions.q: In Hindu iconography, the serpent Vasuki is most often associated with which of the great gods?
questions.explanation: Vasuki is intimately associated with Shiva, around whose neck he is shown coiled as an ornament.

questions.q: Vasuki is regarded as a 'king' among which kind of mythological beings?
questions.explanation: Vasuki is titled the king of serpents, a sovereign among the Nagas.

questions.q: In the Samudra Manthana, which side held the head (mouth) of the serpent Vasuki while churning?
questions.explanation: The asuras held Vasuki's head while the devas held his tail, and the fumes from his mouth wearied the demons.

questions.q: According to the Puranas, which deity supported sinking Mount Mandara as a tortoise while Vasuki churned the ocean?
questions.explanation: Vishnu took the form of the tortoise Kurma to bear Mount Mandara on his back during the churning.

questions.q: In Hindu cosmology, the Nagas such as Vasuki are most often said to dwell in which realm?
questions.explanation: The Nagas, including Vasuki, are described as dwelling in Patala, the underworld realms beneath the earth.

questions.q: The serpent Vasuki is often named alongside which other great cosmic serpent of Hindu tradition?
questions.explanation: Vasuki and Ananta Shesha are two of the greatest of the Nagas frequently mentioned together in the Puranas.

questions.q: Because Shiva held the poison disgorged by Vasuki in his throat, by which epithet is Shiva known?
questions.explanation: The poison turned Shiva's throat blue, earning him the name Nilakantha, the blue-throated one.

questions.q: In the Puranas, what supreme goal were the devas and asuras seeking when they churned the ocean using Vasuki?
questions.explanation: The churning, with Vasuki as the rope, was undertaken chiefly to obtain the amrita, the nectar of immortality.

questions.q: In the great churning, which deity is described as serving as Vasuki's tail-end, held by the devas?
questions.explanation: The devas grasped Vasuki's tail while the asuras held his head, alternately pulling to churn the ocean.

questions.q: Vasuki, the serpent king, belongs to a race that in Hindu lore is frequently associated with which element and gift?
questions.explanation: The Nagas, including Vasuki, are linked to waters and the underworld and are guardians of hidden treasure.

questions.q: In the churning episode, the fumes and breath issuing from the serpent Vasuki's mouth chiefly afflicted whom?
questions.explanation: The Puranas relate that the asuras, holding Vasuki's head, were scorched and wearied by the fumes from his mouth.

questions.q: The episode in which Vasuki serves as the churning rope, the Samudra Manthana, is narrated in which body of texts?
questions.explanation: The Samudra Manthana, featuring Vasuki, is recounted in the Mahabharata and several Puranas such as the Bhagavata and Vishnu Puranas.

questions.q: In Hindu iconography of Shiva, the serpent Vasuki is most commonly shown adorning which part of the god?
options: His wrist as a bangle only
questions.explanation: Vasuki is most characteristically depicted coiled around Shiva's neck as a living ornament.

questions.q: Which of these is NOT a role traditionally ascribed to the serpent Vasuki in Hindu mythology?
options: The rope in churning the ocean
The mount upon which Vishnu sleeps
questions.explanation: It is Ananta Shesha, not Vasuki, upon whose coils Vishnu reclines in cosmic slumber.

questions.q: In Hindu genealogies of the Nagas, the serpents such as Vasuki and Shesha are most often said to be the offspring of which sage and his consort?
questions.explanation: The Puranas describe the Nagas, including Vasuki, as sons of the sage Kashyapa by his wife Kadru.

questions.q: In the Mahabharata's account of Janamejaya's snake sacrifice, Vasuki's sister is the mother of which sage who halts the sacrifice?
questions.explanation: Vasuki's sister Jaratkaru bore the sage Astika, who intervened to stop Janamejaya's serpent sacrifice and save the Nagas.

questions.q: In the Mahabharata, the serpent Vasuki gives his sister in marriage to a sage who shares her brother's people's fate; what was that sage's name?
questions.explanation: Vasuki gave his sister, also named Jaratkaru, in marriage to the ascetic sage Jaratkaru, and their son was Astika.

questions.q: In Tantric and Puranic symbolism, the coiling of the serpent Vasuki around Shiva's neck is often read as representing what?
options: Shiva's mastery over death and primal energy
questions.explanation: The serpent on Shiva symbolises his mastery over time, death, and the coiled primal energy he holds in perfect control.

questions.q: According to the Puranas, after the halahala from Vasuki was neutralized, the churning of the ocean finally yielded which physician-deity bearing the nectar?
questions.explanation: Dhanvantari, the divine physician, arose from the churned ocean carrying the pot of amrita.

questions.q: In several Puranic accounts, the serpent Vasuki is appointed to encircle and bind which sacred mountain or being as a girdle?
questions.explanation: Vasuki's most celebrated binding is around Mount Mandara, serving as the rope for the cosmic churning.

questions.q: The Nagas led by Vasuki are described in the Mahabharata as fearing destruction in a great fire-sacrifice ordered by which king?
questions.explanation: King Janamejaya conducted the snake sacrifice to avenge his father, threatening Vasuki's race until Astika intervened.

questions.q: In the broader symbolism of the Samudra Manthana, Vasuki the churning-rope, together with the devas and asuras pulling, is often interpreted as representing what truth?
options: That the gods always act alone
That the demons alone created the nectar
That the ocean held nothing of value
That great rewards come only through sustained cooperative effort and endurance
questions.explanation: The churning, with Vasuki as the rope pulled by both sides, is read as a teaching that lasting good arises from patient, combined effort.
